image_prompt: SVG: dual-axis line graph of smartwatch data. X-axis Time / min (0-15, ticks every 1). Left Y-axis Breathing rate / breaths per minute (0-50, ticks every 10), teal line. Right Y-axis Pulse rate / beats per minute (0-150, ticks 0,30,60,90,120,150), orange line. Breathing rises from ~16 (rest, 0-3 min) to a 39 plateau (8-10 min) then falls back to 16 by 14 min; pulse rises from ~72 to a ~120 plateau (5-10 min) then falls back to 72.

SVG: scatter graph of germination vs light intensity. X-axis Light intensity / % (0-100, ticks every 10). Y-axis Number of germinated seeds (0-12, ticks every 2). Species Y (teal crosses): (0,1)(10,2)(20,2)(30,3)(40,4)(50,4)(60,5)(70,7)(80,9)(90,10)(100,10). Species Z (orange dots): (0,1)(10,1)(20,1)(30,2)(40,3)(50,3)(60,4)(70,6)(80,8)(90,8)(100,8). Species Y consistently equals or exceeds Z; both plateau at high light intensity.

SVG: hierarchical classification tree of six big cat species. Coloured horizontal bars stacked top-to-bottom for Kingdom (Animals), Phylum (Chordates), Class (Mammals), Order (Carnivores), Family (Felines), branching into four Genus boxes (Panthera, Neofelis, Acinonyx, Puma) each above a Species box, with arrows linking each rank to the next.

SVG: side-view illustrations of six big cats labelled with common name and italic binomial: Jaguar (Panthera onca), Tiger (Panthera tigris), Lion (Panthera leo), Clouded leopard (Neofelis nebulosa), Cheetah (Acinonyx jubatus), Cougar (Puma concolor). Distinctive markings: rosettes on jaguar, stripes on tiger, mane and tuft on lion, cloud pattern on clouded leopard, tear marks plus solid spots on cheetah, plain coat on cougar.

SVG: colour-coded DNA sequence table for three species (Lion, Tiger, Jaguar), 10 nucleotide columns each; A=green, T=pink, G=yellow, C=blue. Lion: G C G T A T C C C A. Tiger: G C A T A A G G T A. Jaguar: G T G T A T G C C A.

SVG: anatomical diagram of gas exchange in the lungs. Lungs at top with trachea and bronchi; below, a magnified alveolus (sac) surrounded by a U-shaped capillary. Blue arrow shows inhaled air (O₂) entering, red arrow shows exhaled air (CO₂) leaving. Gas-exchange arrows cross the alveolar membrane in both directions. Key: red filled circle = oxygenated blood cell, blue filled circle = deoxygenated blood cell. Caption: Diagram not to scale.

SVG: data table of blood-sample results for four healthy males (Alex, Denzel, Imad, Victor). Rows: White blood cells / per mm³ (6500, 4500, 5200, 9500); Red blood cells / per mm³ (4 800 000, 5 500 000, 4 900 000, 3 300 000); Platelets / per mm³ (250 000, 260 000, 240 000, 270 000). Clean ruled table, shaded header row and label column.

SVG: germination chamber experiment apparatus. Glass tank lit from above by a lamp; bed of seeds at the bottom. Left controller is a thermometer (temperature) with probe entering tank; right controller is a five-position light-intensity dial labelled 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100% with pointer.
SVG: data table — Number of seeds germinated (n=10 seeds per trial) for three species A, B, C at light intensities 100, 75, 50, 25, 0 %. Values: A {10,7,4,2,1}; B {8,6,3,1,0}; C {7,6,7,6,6}.
Original crop is question text only (Q4 intro about high-rise buildings and natural light). No diagram to recreate.

SVG: plant growth chamber experiment. Glass tank with four young green plants in soil under a lamp. Left controller shows Temperature 30°C with thermometer probe entering tank; right controller is a light-intensity dial with five settings 0%, 30%, 60%, 90%, 100%.

SVG: plant growth chamber as in page-31 (four plants under lamp, Temperature controller and Light-intensity dial), plus a 0–10 cm ruler beneath the tank with mm minor ticks for measuring plant heights.

SVG: four labelled plant trials (Trial 1–Trial 4) of slightly different heights growing in a glass chamber. A draggable vertical 0–10 cm ruler with mm minor ticks sits to the left for measuring heights. Captions: Light intensity 30%, Temperature 30°C.
Original crop is question text only (Q5 numbered method for temperature experiment). No diagram to recreate.

SVG: bird-banding 'THEN' slide. Dark navy intro box explaining bird banding (plastic/metal rings on a bird's leg, unique number or colour sequence). Pink 'THEN' label, paragraph about 23 birds banded in 1902 with aluminium rings, only 1 returned from 89 km away. Illustration of a small brown warbler-like bird perched on a branch with a metal band on its leg, plus a magnified circle showing the band close-up on the leg.

SVG: infographic of bird population changes for four habitat groups, each in its own downward arrow tile with habitat-coloured background and a representative bird silhouette. -23 % Arctic tundra birds (snowy/grey, snow bunting). -33 % Forest birds (green forest, evening grosbeak yellow+black). -37 % Shorebirds (grey beach, plover with long legs). -53 % Grassland birds (green grass, bobolink black with cream nape).
